controller_messages: I checked README.md and git status after Claude finished.

README says cc-manager is an interactive terminal app that puts a Codex controller in front of Claude Code. The controller either answers directly or delegates work, then reviews the repo state and decides whether to continue or stop.

Setup is Node 18.17+ with npm install and npm link. Main usage is either the interactive cc-manager shell or one-shot commands like run, resume, status, logs, list, and doctor. Git status shows existing run-log files and local changes in src/claude.js and src/process-utils.js, but no source edits from this summary request.
stop_reason: Verified the README summary and no further Claude work is needed.
